fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Montana Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Montana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity montana, how to transfer occupational therapist license to montana, occupational therapist compact montana, montana occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Montana Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
board.mailing_address: 301 S Park Ave, P.O. Box 200513, Helena MT 59620-0513

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Montana.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account & Start Online Application
steps.description: Navigate to the Montana Professional Licensing E-Portal (https://ebiz.mt.gov/POL/) and register for a new user account. Once logged in, select 'Start a New Application' for an Occupational Therapist license and complete the required fields.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee as part of your online application through the Montana Professional Licensing E-Portal.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts verifying your occupational therapy degree to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Board of Occupational Therapy Practice.

steps.title: Verify NBCOT Exam Results
steps.description: Ensure your NBCOT (National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy) exam results are verifiable by the Board; typically, you'll need to arrange for NBCOT to send verification directly to the Montana Board.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check, which may involve using specific vendors or law enforcement agencies.

steps.title: Provide Verification of Licensure (for Endorsement)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement and you hold or have held an OT license in another state, arrange for verification of licensure to be sent directly from each state's licensing board to the Montana Board of Occupational Therapy Practice.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Montana offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Montana Board of Occupational Therapy Practice for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Montana is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Montana without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Montana also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Montana OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $200. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Montana have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Montana has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Montana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Montana is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Montana without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Montana also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Montana
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Montana, navigate to the Montana Professional Licensing E-Portal, create an account, and complete the online application.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Navigate to the Montana Professional Licensing E-Portal (https://ebiz.mt.gov/POL/). 2. Click 'Register' to create a new user account if you do not have one. 3. Follow the prompts to set up your account with a username and password. 4. Once logged in, select 'Start a New Application' for an Occupational Therapist license.
